text:
Yala National Park
If viewing a diverse array of wildlife is at the top of your agenda then Yala national park is the place to visit. The vegetation consists of scrub jungle, …
Beautiful national park with numerous animals! Water buffalo, elephants, leopards, monkeys, etc…

Huge park with various species of birds and animals.
Very pretty and well-maintained.
Entrance fee: 10,000 Rs
Extremely crowded, even though we were somehow in the off-season - end of February, columns of cars waiting to see a leopard that was nowhere to be found.
Main entrace for yala block 1

A large and diverse nature reserve with water bodies, open areas and an area that is forested/dense. We did an afternoon walk of about 3 hours until almost sunset. A very large variety of birds and waterfowl and animals in general. We saw …

Not really sure how anyone can give this 4 or 5 stars.
The Safari starts with a queue at the main entrance with a long line of Toyota Jeeps, it is impossible to count them but there is a least 100, all diesel and …

A beautiful park with abundant wildlife. Spotting animals in a park is always a bit of a gamble, but the guides are experienced and do their best. We were lucky. The jeeps are definitely better spread out, and there seem to be significantly fewer of them than in other parks. Entrance fees are high, as is typical.

Having mixed feelings about my experience there, I'll tell you what was great and what was awful in my opinion. We went there early in the morning with a few people, thinking we'd avoid the crowds, but right after entering the park, we were …

One of the best budget resorts I’ve visited. The place is very beautiful, calm, and peaceful. The staff are friendly and the safari arrangements were excellent. The surroundings are full of greenery and nature, which makes it very relaxing. …
Adventure safari
Yala is more than just its wildlife; it’s a place brimming with natural beauty. Capturing its essence is a challenge, but being connected to nature brings the moment to life. …
Water buffalo, a rabbit, 2 stray dogs, and hundreds of jeeps on our trip

Avoid this !!!
Our driver was using drugs, and giving it to other drivers. While driving the jeep with 7 of us in it. …
A disappointing experience; admittedly beautiful scenery and some lovely animals, but far too many jeeps all concentrated in the same places – not recommended after Kruger 😉 …

There are so many jeeps, the poor animals don't know where to hide.
The safari area is limited; we just drove in circles around the same places. The rest of the roads are closed to the public. …

Yala National Park was an amazing experience. Seeing wild elephants and spotting a leopard in its natural habitat was unforgettable. The safari felt real and adventurous, especially in the early morning. Definitely a must-visit place if you love wildlife and nature.

Yala national park is grat place to visit. Although familiar with a few of the more common animals here which we saw such elephant peafowl water Buffalo spotted deer crocodiles it was the first time seeing some of these creatures in life and not on a docum...
A large park with many jeeps, but there's still plenty to see. We were lucky enough to see a leopard too.
Guide was incredible. It seemed like the wildlife was everywhere. Saw two leopards, male elephant, peacocks everywhere, biodiversity at the watering hole was incredible. Beautiful place. …
The park is beautiful, you can drive right up to the entrance in a car and rent a jeep... There are a lot of them parked in front of the ticket office, they cost from 10 to 15 thousand per car.

Good Park. Very big. A lot of elephants. Leopard chasing can be tiring. Big junctions whenever someone spots Leopard. And they mostly sleep high on the trees. For lunch they drive you by the ocean for a 2 hour picnic and break and that is quite awesome.

Предмет: Complaint Regarding Yala National Park Safari Experience

I am extremely disappointed with my safari experience at Yala National Park from …
My wife and I paid 17,000 rupees for the safari and 23,000 rupees for the entrance fee. We picked up at 5 a.m. and returned to our lodging around 10 a.m. …

Nice national park that seems to be well taken care of as I could see. Visitors are not allowed to exit the cars and walk freely whoch is very good for protecting the animals habitats! We saw amongst other thing, elephants, buffalos, …

I highly recommend taking the morning safari in Yala National Park. I was told that the afternoon safari can be more challenging due to the intense heat, which often causes the animals to seek shelter and makes them harder to spot. In my …
First things first, on a safari it’s a gamble on what you can see, if you’re looking for a gaurentee animal sighting first time then visit a zoo. …

Main Entrance (Palatupana) is convenient and with easy access for Yala safari. It can be fully crowded and may involve waiting in lines specially in morning and evening time. We should take tickets to enter the national park. It’s better planning your timing (early morning is best) tends to make the experience more enjoyable
A great wildlife safari. It’s not Africa, but it’s not bad either.

Yala National Park was a good experience and definitely worth visiting. We saw quite a number of animals, which made the safari enjoyable. It’s important to get a good vehicle with proper suspension and a driver who is willing to provide a …
Saw 4 leopards and tons of elephants! Amazing experience - I recommend a morning tour :)

A beautiful place.
Downside: unfortunately, it's loud and full of jeeps.
The park is beautiful and huge, the animals are around so you can be lucky and see them, but mass tourism is very disappointing and the jeep drivers make it worse. …

It was a rally safari. Almost 400 jeeps at 6 am in the main entrance, mostly of them with only 2 passengers. They should limit the number of jeeps to ensure calm and good conditions for the natural park. …
You must visit experience tour guide otherwise you will be disappointed

Amazing experience!
Easy access and very courteous staff
#yalanationalpark #srilankanleopards #wildlifesrilanka

It wasn't a zip safari, but a zip drive. I was stuck in traffic for an hour, unable to move, because I saw a leopard, which was barely visible. The next morning, I went to the market and was happy to see more animals.

Very dissapointing experience due to the amount of vehicles on the roads at once. It seems to be more about the money than the experience of sharing the beautiful animals.
Saw more on the public roads.

I was expecting a more exciting safari, so I booked a full-day trip. In reality, a half-day trip is sufficient, and there's almost no point in spending the night in Yala just for that. It's easier to take a tour from the coast.
Very good
A must see while in Sri Lanca
Must go

Far too many jeeps in one place. We had a really unpleasant experience with our guide and an elephant, apparently named Gamunu. The elephant was walking on the road where we wanted to go, and instead of stopping or turning around like all …

Yala National Park is incredibly disappointing. While we got to see an Elephant and some Crocodiles the mass tourism just destroys the entire experience.
You will be waiting in the smog of 1000 engines in lines of cars to not move at …

Many hours wasted standing around, too many jeeps making noise, and all you see are elephants, peacocks, crocodiles, and little else. It wasn't worth the early start (they picked us up at 4:30 am, only for the tour to start at 9 am) and …
Better book online, its separate queue.
That was okay, but we mostly saw animals when we left, even though we were inside for 3 hours.
Amazing
Wonderful place for kids
It's a beautiful park but you spend most of the time in jeep traffic jams. Given the price you pay it's an over priced jeep ride.

During a half-day safari, we spotted six elephants: twice a mother with her calf, and twice two lone male elephants. We were told that one of them was very aggressive. We spent a long time searching for a leopard, and finally, close to …

A wonderful park in terms of both landscapes and animals. No one should go to this park expecting to see leopards. It's more about the overall flora and fauna. The large number of visitors and jeeps is a downside, making it feel more like …
Lots of wild life. Beautiful environment. 10/10
We didn't see any leopards, but maybe he saw us:)) There were elephants, reptiles, and birds. I liked it.

Yala National Park is one of Sri Lanka’s most captivating natural destinations, offering an unforgettable wildlife experience for travelers seeking adventure and breathtaking scenery. The park is known for its remarkable biodiversity, …
vary good

You should plan on spending about half a day there.

The restrooms at the rest area on the beach are unfortunately not well-equipped …
Everything was great, we saw elephants, a lot of birds, crocodiles, deer, buffalo, and even a leopard.
Great place to see animals. The roads could be better kept. Also the only toilets on site were a disgrace.

We saw many different animals and once a leopard in the bushes in the distance, but we were surrounded by dozens of jeeps.
On the way back, we finally took a nap on a small road, and what did we suddenly …
Great tour with lots of animals and a leopard.
It really was a great experience and we enjoyed every minute!
Needed a wee
Spectacular, we went in the afternoon from 2 to 6 and we were able to see deer, jackals, 2 leopards, deer, elephants, crocodiles, Sri Lankan chickens, a bear... incredible, yes, it was very hot.
Best safari 🥰🥰 …
Very good experience, indeed we can see leopards

An incredible experience in Yala. We went with Back of Beyond, a super guide. I understand you can also go without a guide and pay less. Unfortunately, we didn't see any leopards because it was September, but we did see plenty of animals. I highly recommend it if you're near Yala; it's an adventure I won't forget.

Late afternoon spent in the park searching for animals. Our driver was doing his best, but I was disappointed by the number of jeeps in the park. Since they all seem to know which animals are there, everyone goes to the same place, causing …
National park with various species of animals that we encountered right at the entrance, during the time we spent there we saw all the emblematic animals for less than 3 hours
Dreamlike national park with incredible wildlife
So Beautiful ❤️

For us it was a 10 experience, in the first 2 hours of sunrise we were lucky to be able to see the 5 of Sri Lanka, buffalo, crocodile, elephant, leopard and bear. Surely it is not always possible but we were lucky. Our guide was a 10, with …

It was a terrible experience. We spent a lot of money but didn't see many worthwhile animals. We set off at 5:30 am, arrived at the ticket office at 6:12 am, but it closed at 9:30 am, including a 40-minute traffic jam. Our actual safari …

A nice safari experience. You need to hire a jeep to inside the park. There is one zone that allows private vehicles. We were not fortunate enough to see any leopard. Otherwise we saw a lot of animals like elephants, fox, buffaloes, deers, …

It is so bad! There were absolutely no animals to see in this park. The species and numbers were few and far between. You'd have to take for a long time to see one. There were traffic jams and no one to maintain order. We spent a lot of money but didn't see any worthwhile animals. It was terrible. I don't recommend this park.

We saw the leopard at a distance on July 26, 2025. Additionally we saw were the crocodiles, buffoloes, monkeys,deer,and the elephants.
There is order in the entrance. There is a cafeteria if anyone wants to purchase breakfast.

The main entrance to Yala National Park is well-organized and gives you a warm welcome to one of Sri Lanka’s most famous wildlife destinations. Ticketing and entry procedures are smooth, and there are clear signboards with useful …
This is where all the safari cars line up to get in on time.
Great experience. Highly recommended.
Opened on time!
Landscape is amazing. The chance to see more animals is higher in early morning, like 6AM!

Good tour.
I didn't appreciate the guide that is provided starts by telling you they expect a tip at the end, before you even start.

Maybe because we went during the off-season, there weren't many people. But the locals said that the peak season here is around May!
I didn't see any leopards. …
Very beautiful place

The landscapes are very beautiful, but it's true that like the Google reviews, I have mixed feelings about the experience. Indeed, we saw a few elephants (4 for us), lots of buffalo, deer, does, birds, a crocodile.... We were able to see the leopard hiding very quickly because it was surrounded by 30 jeeps, so meh
I visited Yala National Park in March 2025. We saw many birds (bee-eaters, kingfishers, peacocks, eagles, etc.). Unfortunately, there were no leopards. However, we did see elephants. …
Amazing place as always.
Excellent

It’s funny cause during the way from Weligama to Yala, we saw more animals then in the Yala National Park during paid Safari 😀🤷‍♂️
I mean: …

Don't go here with high hopes. It is very dense and difficult to spot wildlife from the tracks, but the most disappointing aspect was that guides do not go very far into the park and our morning was spent covering a very small area and …
Good park.
We had an amazing guide, Kamesh who was very knowledgeable and helpful with finding all the animals. We had a great time at the national park and highly recommended the trip.
It is a bird watcher paradise. Disappointed that we could not view a leopard.

I loved doing this safari but I have to give it a four star and not five because there is a overcrowding problem with the jeeps. As opposed to other safaris, I have been.This place allows several jeeps up to 10 to corral the animals just to …

A mass of greenery that completely obscures the park's animals.
You can just glimpse some buffalo, deer, and peacocks, but otherwise it's a total disappointment, with just a hint of movement in the undergrowth, though …

If you are after wildlife go to the Masai Mara. Yala is ok-ish if you run out of things to do in SriLanka, but not worth a dedicated trip. During the ‘safari’ you’ll drive for about four hours on potholed roads to spot some elephants, deer and rabbits.
Jeep ride was fun. Paid LKR 12,000 through a 3rd party driver and around LKR 24,000 for 2 foreigners at the gate. …
AVOID THIS! I would definitely not do it again. It’s not a safari but a jeep tour. We didn’t see any elephants or leopards, only a few birds, a buffalo, and a deer and 40-50 cars... …

I liked the park itself, it is very large, beautiful wildlife, we even saw a few animals. But, the organization is at the lowest level. The car turned out to be faulty, we got stuck, they pulled us on a tow truck. Then we started it on the …

The place is worth visiting. Book a jeep in advance - you will save money. On average - 15,000 rupees per jeep. We were on a morning safari, saw many animals and birds. We did not see a cheetah, only tracks
Half a day at Yala Safari Park, painted with safari vehicles in a small park. We saw a leopard, but from a distance and after queuing for 30 minutes.

An unforgettable experience. We saw a lot of animals: from elephants to mongooses and peacocks. You can see many species of birds, butterflies and ungulates. The toilet on the way looks like a horse barn.... The stench, the dirt.... The …
Must be prepared for long lines especially in the morning

A great experience.
Crowded compared to other safaris but it's easy to see animals.
This is the worst safari I have ever been on. 1.5 elephants, 3 deer and a few peacocks, 3.5 hours spent on it. And about an hour stuck in a traffic jam of jeeps...
Safari to do! You will see elephants, leopards, crocodiles and many other wild animals

Shocked by people's behavior towards this magnificent natural environment. It's an absolute business center, miles away from the idea of ​​a national park where animals should be protected and where humans live in harmony with nature. The …

It was nice, but didn't worth the money or waking up before 5 in the morning.
There are just too many vehicles trying to get in at the same time.
The actual chances of seeing animals that you can't see outside of the reserve, …
What an awesome experience! Got the wonderful opportunity to see the leopard. Also the beauty of the nature and the variety of animals. A must visit place for all…

I recently went on a safari in Yala, Sri Lanka, and it was disappointing. The experience felt chaotic from start to finish. The jeeps were a lot, some of which were very old, were driving in all directions, creating long lines and …
Would not recommend. Mainly because the animals hide in the very rich vegetation. Roads are bad and crowded by many jeeps that just queue. …

Worst safari experience I’ve ever had in my life! Got to see no wild animals mostly. And it’s famous for bears and leopards which I didn’t get to see even after spending loads of time and money! A complete waste of time and energy please don’t visit!!!
It's great if you go first thing in the morning, around 6 am, and you're one of the first there. As the morning goes on, it gets harder to get around because of the number of cars.

Worst safari park I have ever visited.
You won’t see anything apart from few elephants, peacocks, buffalos and few species of birds. …
Vary good

The beautiful landscape, lots of birds, especially in the wetlands and lagoons..some elephants and the distant silhouette of the leopard crossing the road.
The toilets in a deplorable state, no one has cleaned them in weeks, no paper or soap.
A coffee 1000 Rup
The safari park is worth the money, but you shouldn't go in with the big tour operators. I had my own guide with a large vehicle.

An impressive national park with a wide variety of animals. We didn't see any leopards, but there were elephants, buffalo, crocodiles, and many different kinds of birds living together in the same ecosystem. My only complaint is the …

We booked a tour with Imperial Jeep Safari Tours for 145,000 LKR (6 people). Pickup was scheduled for Monday morning at 4:00 a.m. When we called to ask where the driver was, we were told there were "car problems"; a driver would be at the …

An enjoyable day driving around rough tracks trying to spot wildlife.
A full day tour is a bit long. Between 1130 & 1400hrs, all vehicles have to leave the tracks & congregate at the beach or the river, so you loose quite a few …
Beware of the NEW prices for Yala which aren’t shown before you arrive - 18% Tax + $10 service charge isn’t right

They have a lot of respect for animals. You can see many different species quite well. The downside is the number of jeeps, as they call to each other and crowd together to see any elephant or leopard sighting.

Big place.
Beautiful.
Always choose morning for Safari. …
Yala is a wonderful place to spot leopard 🐆 and bear 🐻. We witnessed all of them including elephants 🐘 …
Our tour guide and driver bought tickets online in advance. This allowed us to get on the safari much faster than the offline queue. I highly recommend it.

Great safari
Great guide
Great preservation of nature and animals in it, feels untouched

It was very exciting to observe the animals. We expected more, but that's probably uncontrollable. Our driver, Sempath, was excellent. He was always up to date and had the latest leopard and elephant spots we wanted to see on his radar. He …
We were quite disappointed; few animals, a long time in the jeep, and our guide's service was poor.
Way to crowded, this is not in the wellbeing of the animals. The unfortunate result of mass tourism
Unfortunately, we didn't see many animals, and when we did, we couldn't get any further with the Jeep because they were all there.
Good place
Fairly good develop entrance to park with lot of tourists attractions...
Beautiful national park with lots of animals. We managed to see lots of elephants, buffalo, birds and one leopard. Definitely worth a visit.

Terrible safari.  The drivers have no clue how to attract game for viewing. They drove like idiots down the main entry road. I thought there must be something special they all trying to get to 1st. No, they just have no clue. Any game would …
Beutiful amazing place!
Must go very early to avoid the crowds and noon time heat/dust but must be lucky to both get a good driver/guide as well be lucky to see the wildlife.
Best had a good safari.

An absolute must-see! The park is fantastic, with a wide variety of animals—beautiful birds, crocodiles, water buffalo, elephants, and even leopards. The views from the beach, rocks, and woods are stunning, and the ponds with beautiful …
Good to know: you can pay for your entrance tickets with a debit card. No cash needed, it was very quick.

Beautiful park with sunrise 👍, when the jeeps split up it's great, but as soon as the jeeps start hunting for a leopard or elephant, whoever gets to see it better is a massacre 🥹, however, you see animals in the wild and that's the greatest experience …

Unfortunately, I had overestimated the capabilities of this national park.

Around 6 a.m., just before the park gates open, nearly 100 jeeps gather on the …

The place is utterly incredible. From my experience, you are guaranteed to see something which, in its natural habitat doing its own thing, can’t be underestimated. I was lucky enough to see the leopard, bear, elephants, mongoose, fox, …

Amazing Wildlife Experience!

Yala National Park is an absolute gem for wildlife enthusiasts and nature …
Sri Lanka's largest wildlife park is located in Yala. In this place, you can see wild animals like elephants, deer, crocodiles, hyenas, peacocks, leopards, etc. in the forest in the early morning.

The main entrance to park from Kirinda side where the tickets are checked. There's an unnecessary delay due manual tickets issuance and verification. Can easily digitized and sped up the process e.g. QR code based tickets.
Incredible experience, I'll never forget it. Although we didn't see the leopard, we enjoyed it immensely.
It was a good place to see many animals but the road was so bad and there were no animals when it was raining
I interested
Recommending anyone that to buy tickets in advance.

Great, great, great
But it's best to only wear old clothes
Afterwards, everything will be stained by red dust
Pretty fun

If it’s rainy and with heavy rain like for the past 3 days they should advise people not to go in or close the park. It’s unviable. Roads are blocked by water and you don’t see animals as they don’t come out in the rain. Please be mindful: …

A safari in the back of a jeep in a national park. In the first phase, a leopard hunt, when twenty jeeps with tourists jostle for the best view of the leopard, which eventually passed between the cars and was visible. Then a leisurely ride …

Good place to start Yala safari.
Entrance start at 6am and safaris end at 6pm. It possible to do full day safaris or half day safaris. For half days, safari start at 2pm. I would recommend going in the morning as more animals come out for water.
Very nice to visit places
Best place to see leopards, lovely experience
